overrides.override_summary: Certain liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code Section 1671.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages in FA may not hold up in CA courts.

overrides.override_summary: CA Business and Professions Code Sections 20000-20043 provide franchisee rights concerning termination or non-renewal. If FA is inconsistent, law controls.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may provide stronger termination/non-renewal protections than FA.

overrides.override_summary: FA requires NY law application. This may not be enforceable under CA law.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA courts may apply CA franchise law instead of NY law.

overrides.override_summary: FA requires venue in Fairfax County, VA. This may not be enforceable under CA law.
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate in CA courts.

overrides.override_summary: General release required on renewal/transfer. CA Corporations Code 31512 voids waiver of rights under Franchise Investment Law. Business and Professions Code 20010 voids waiver under Franchise Relations Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Release cannot waive CA franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: General release language shall not relieve franchisor from liability imposed by HI franchise laws.
overrides.why_it_matters: HI law claims survive any general release.

overrides.override_summary: IL Franchise Disclosure Act governs for IL licensees. Provisions re: governing law, jurisdiction, venue, jury waiver do not waive IL FDD Act rights.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law cannot be contracted around.

overrides.override_summary: IL FDD Act Section 41: any provision purporting to waive compliance with the Act is void (except negotiated settlements with independent counsel).
overrides.why_it_matters: Anti-waiver protection for IL franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: MD franchise law may supersede FA in areas of termination and renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD law may provide additional protections.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in MD for claims arising under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year statute of limitations from grant.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can litigate in MD for MD law claims.

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not release franchisor from liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. No statement/questionnaire shall waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong anti-waiver/anti-disclaimer protection in MD.

overrides.override_summary: Provision permitting termination before expiration except for good cause is VOID. Good cause requires failure to comply with lawful FA provision + written notice + reasonable opportunity to cure (up to 30 days).
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be terminated without good cause and cure opportunity.

overrides.override_summary: Provision refusing renewal without fair compensation (FMV of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, furnishings) is VOID — but only if term <5 years AND franchisee prohibited from operating under another mark AND does not receive 6 months advance notice.
overrides.why_it_matters: Limited renewal protection for short-term franchises.

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration or litigation outside MI is VOID (but franchisee may agree to out-of-state arbitration at time of proceeding).
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in VA/NY.

overrides.override_summary: Requirement to assent to release, waiver, etc. depriving franchisee of MI Franchise Investment Law rights is VOID. Does not preclude post-agreement settlements.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchise law rights cannot be waived by contract.

overrides.override_summary: MN Statutes Section 80C.14 requires 90 days notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days notice of non-renewal, except certain specified cases.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN provides significantly longer notice and cure periods than FA.

overrides.override_summary: MN Rule 2860.4400J prohibits requiring franchisee to consent to liquidated damages.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provisions in FA are unenforceable in MN.

overrides.override_summary: MN Statutes 80C.21 and Rule 2860.4400J prohibit requiring litigation outside MN, requiring jury trial waiver, or requiring consent to termination penalties or judgment notes.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees litigate in MN and keep all statutory rights/remedies.

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not relieve franchisor from MN Franchise Investment Law liability.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN law claims survive any general release.

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchise Law requires NY law govern causes of action arising under it. NY General Business Law Article 33 (Sections 680-695) may supersede inconsistent FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law is self-enforcing and cannot be contracted around.

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY courts may not enforce mandatory releases.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law. Franchisor may only assign to an assignee willing and able to assume obligations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee has statutory termination rights; assignment protections.

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions in FA are deleted in their entirety.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees face no liquidated damages exposure.

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes FA/NY law if in conflict. FA governed by ND law.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law controls, not NY law.

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction/venue outside ND is DELETED. Jury trial waiver is DELETED.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees litigate in ND with jury trial right preserved.

overrides.override_summary: Release language shall not relieve franchisor from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims survive releases.

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal will not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI law claims survive releases.

overrides.override_summary: Any provision restricting jurisdiction/venue outside RI or requiring non-RI law is VOID for RI Franchise Investment Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate in RI under RI law.

overrides.override_summary: VA Retail Franchising Act Section 13.1-564: unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause. Grounds not constituting 'reasonable cause' under VA law may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: VA provides reasonable cause requirement for termination.

overrides.override_summary: WA RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA re: termination and renewal. Court decisions may also supersede.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law may provide additional termination/renewal protections.

overrides.override_summary: Release will not include WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel after FA in effect. Unreasonable limitations on statute of limitations or jury trial rights may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong protection for WA franchisee rights.

overrides.override_summary: In event of conflict, WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law controls over NY law for WA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site must be in WA or mutually agreed. Franchisee may bring action in WA for franchise sale claims or WA Act violations.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can litigate in WA.

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year and independent contractors earning <$250K/year (adjusted for inflation). No-hire/no-solicit provisions between franchisees or between franchisee and franchisor employees are VOID.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA severely limits noncompete enforcement and prohibits no-poach provisions.

overrides.override_summary: All claims settled by arbitration (AAA/JAMS), venue Fairfax County VA, seat NY NY. Franchisor may elect court for temporary/injunctive relief.
overrides.why_it_matters: Mandatory arbitration (unique among all states in this FDD).
